source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ABC=90^\circ$, $\angle A=60^\circ$. Point $D$ is on side $AC$ with $\angle DBC=30^\circ$ and $AC=12\,\text{cm}$. What is the perimeter of $\triangle ABD$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $\triangle ABC\cong \triangle ADE$, and $\angle D=30^\circ$,\\
it follows that $\angle B=\angle D=30^\circ$,\\
Since $\angle C=70^\circ$,\\
then $\angle BAC=180^\circ-\angle B-\angle C=180^\circ-70^\circ-30^\circ=80^\circ$,\\
Since $\triangle ABC\cong \triangle ADE$,\\
it follows that $\angle EAD=\angle BAC=80^\circ$,\\
Also, given that $\angle CAD=35^\circ$,\\
then $\angle EAC=\angle EAD-\angle DAC=80^\circ-35^\circ=45^\circ$,\\
Thus, the answer is: $\boxed{B}$.

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ABC=90^\circ$, $\angle A=60^\circ$. Point $D$ is on side $AC$ with $\angle DBC=30^\circ$ and $AC=12\,\text{cm}$. What is the perimeter of $\triangle ABD$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text: \textbf{Solution:} Since $\triangle ABC\cong \triangle ADE$, and $\angle D=30^\circ$, it follows that $\angle B=\angle D=30^\circ$. Since $\angle C=70^\circ$, then $\angle BAC=180^\circ-\angle B-\angle C=180^\circ-70^\circ-30^\circ=80^\circ$. Since $\triangle ABC\cong \triangle ADE$, it follows that $\angle EAD=\angle BAC=80^\circ$. Also, given that $\angle CAD=35^\circ$, then $\angle EAC=\angle EAD-\angle DAC=80^\circ-35^\circ=45^\circ$. Thus, the answer is: $\boxed{B}$.
